{{Infobox location
| name = Blackflame Empire
| introduced = Soulsmith
}}

''See also: [[Blackflame Empire Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] is a vast territory located far to the east, characterized by a highly organized society where everything is categorized and ranked. Historically, the empire was founded and ruled for centuries by the [[Blackflame family]], who maintained control through overwhelming force and a destructive [[madra]] that mimicked black [[dragons]]. However, this power came at a high cost; the family's unique [[madra]] eventually consumed them, leading to their decline and near extinction. The original empire also suffered massive devastation caused by the greed of its rulers, who sought powerful weapons that ultimately turned much of the continent into a wasteland. A new version of the empire was later established, currently governed by the [[Naru clan]]. The current leader is [[Emperor Naru Huan]], a powerful human who rules with [[emerald]]-winged majesty. While the empire has seen periods of prosperity under the [[Naru]], it currently faces significant instability due to external threats such as [[dragons]], [[Dreadgods]], and encroaching kingdoms. The empire's military and social structures include specialized groups like the [[Skysworn]], who serve as scouts and defenders. Despite its current vulnerabilities and fractured western regions, the empire remains a significant political entity with influential clans like the [[Jai]] and Arelius families participating in its affairs.}}

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Located far to the east, the [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] is a civilization that serves as the home of the [[Jai clan]]. This territory is characterized by a lack of harvesters similar to those utilized by [[miners]] and features a remote range of mountains along its southern edge. Although the empire contains only a small number of Underlords, [[Eithan]] serves the realm in this capacity. Additionally, the empire is a potential destination for the relocation of [[Jai Long]]'s sister.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is the empire where the [[Jai clan]] resides.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c5">''Soulsmith'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Blackflame Empire]] contains only a handful of Underlords.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c7">''Soulsmith'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a civilization.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a place where they do not have harvesters like the ones used by the [[miners]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c14">''Soulsmith'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is located far to the east.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Eithan]] serves the [[Blackflame Empire]] as an [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c19">''Soulsmith'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a location where [[Jai Long]]'s sister might move.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Blackflame Empire]] contains a remote range of mountains at its southern edge.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c20">''Soulsmith'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Blackflame ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] is a territory composed of various clans that was originally founded by a family discovering a source of [[dragon]] power. Although [[the Emperor]] has struggled to maintain the full scope of this realm for two generations, it remains a destination where [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] are brought by a young [[Underlord]] for training. The empire's western edge is defined by the mountains located to the west of the [[Desolate Wilds]], and its society utilizes a strict system for ranking and categorizing all things. Within this land, the value of resources is immense, such as a single superior-grade scale being worth ten thousand of [[Lindon]]'s scales. While [[Eithan]] is not entirely from this location, his arrival has made a significant impact upon the empire.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">12</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|There are not many who know [[Yerin's master]] in the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c3">''Blackflame'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is the destination [[Lindon]] is returning to.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Blackflame Empire]] contains different clans.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The mountains to the west of the [[Desolate Wilds]] are at the western edge of the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c6">''Blackflame'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a location the characters are landing in.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c7">''Blackflame'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Blackflame Empire]] was founded by a family that found a source of [[dragon]] power.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c8">''Blackflame'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Blackflame Empire]] uses a ranking and categorization system for everything.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a territory that [[the Emperor]] has been unable to hold in its full scope for two generations.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Eithan]] is not entirely from the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c10">''Blackflame'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|In the [[Blackflame Empire]], a superior-grade scale is worth about ten thousand of [[Lindon]]'s scales.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Eithan]]'s arrival had an impact in the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c11">''Blackflame'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A young [[Underlord]] had taken [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] back into the [[Blackflame Empire]] for training.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c21">''Blackflame'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Situated at the southern edge of a lush green expanse, the [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] is an imperial territory owned by the [[Akura clan]] and ruled by [[Naru Huan]]. While it is a land where killing an [[Underlord]] is difficult, the original empire met its demise during the last awakening of the [[Corrupted ones]]. Within this realm, the green armor of the [[Skysworn]] is highly respected, and no resident possesses the space-control abilities held by the [[Akura]] Matriarch or her disciples. During a duel that takes place within its borders, [[Lindon]] finds himself in the southern portion of the empire, a place capable of employing strategies such as fleeing like mice when faced with overwhelming natural disasters.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">12</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a place where killing an [[Underlord]] is difficult.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c1">''Skysworn'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is located at the southern edge of a lush green expanse.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Blackflame Empire]] has met its demise.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c2">''Skysworn'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Lindon]] is in the southern part of the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c3">''Skysworn'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is the empire where the duel takes place.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c4">''Skysworn'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The original [[Blackflame Empire]] was destroyed by the last time [[Corrupted ones]] woke up.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c7">''Skysworn'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Skysworn]]'s green armor is highly respected within the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c8">''Skysworn'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is an empire ruled by an emperor.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c9">''Skysworn'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is led by [[Naru Huan]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c11">''Skysworn'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Blackflame Empire]] can employ a strategy of fleeing like mice in front of overwhelming natural disasters.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a territory owned by the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c14">''Skysworn'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|No one in the [[Blackflame Empire]] possesses the space-control abilities of the [[Akura family Matriarch]] or her disciples.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c14" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] is a political entity where [[Skysworn]] serve as celebrated heroes. The empire's perceived strength grows when an [[Akura]] is in its service, and it possesses a healer capable of restoring hooks. While the empire contains a western labyrinth and serves as the origin point for [[dragons]], it would be reduced to a war-torn desert without the protection of the [[Beast King]] and other guardians. Additionally, a delegation from the empire has been noted near the [[Life Well]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">9</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is an empire where [[Skysworn]] serve as heroes.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c1">''Ghostwater'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Blackflame Empire]]'s strength is perceived to increase if an [[Akura]] works for it.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c2">''Ghostwater'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is mentioned in relation to the [[Skysworn]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c7">''Ghostwater'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a political entity.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Blackflame Empire]] has a healer who can restore hooks.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|Without the [[Beast King]] and other guardians, the [[Blackflame Empire]] would be a war-torn desert.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c12">''Ghostwater'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Blackflame Empire]] contains a western labyrinth.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c14">''Ghostwater'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[dragons]] came from the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Blackflame Empire]] has a delegation near the [[Life Well]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c16">''Ghostwater'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Following the fall of the [[Jai clan]], the [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] existed as a fractured and lawless territory where competition between [[Monarch]] disciples often spilled over. During a selection process, an army bearing various faction symbols streamed through a tall column of darkness to establish a camp behind [[Orthos]]. Although the empire defends the territory within [[Night Wheel Valley]], its thin [[aura]] makes Underlords rare, though they are treated like kings within the realm. After experiencing the much stronger [[aura]] of the valley, the empire's own [[aura]] felt weaker, eventually leading to a retreat. Following these events, [[Akura Charity]] addressed the people of the empire and promised to provide a gift in compensation, though she did not select [[Lindon]] as one of their three competitors.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">22</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Blackflame Empire]] was fractured and lawless following the fall of the [[Jai clan]] and before the rise of the [[Arelius family]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4">''Underlord'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a location where competition between [[Monarch]] disciples may spill over.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Eithan]] spoke of growing together for the good of the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is gathering for a selection process.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c6">''Underlord'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Underlords are rare in the [[Blackflame Empire]] because the [[aura]] is thin.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c7">''Underlord'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The army streaming from the portal bears symbols of different [[Blackflame Empire]] factions.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c8">''Underlord'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Blackflame Empire]] camp is located behind [[Orthos]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Blackflame Empire]] defends the territory within the [[Night Wheel Valley]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c9">''Underlord'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[aura]] in the current location is a hundred times stronger than in the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Seishen Kingdom]] might be attacking the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Blackflame Empire]] uses [[Skysworn]] as scouts.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c11">''Underlord'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Underlords in the [[Blackflame Empire]] are treated like kings.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c12">''Underlord'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is one of the factions [[Mercy]] mentions.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c13">''Underlord'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Blackflame Empire]]'s crest is stamped on the scales [[Eithan]] used for [[Dross]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c14">''Underlord'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Yerin]] intends to attempt her advancement in the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[aura]] in the [[Blackflame Empire]] felt weaker after staying in [[Night Wheel Valley]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c15">''Underlord'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is retreating.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c18">''Underlord'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The portal to the [[Blackflame Empire]] is a tall column of darkness.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c19">''Underlord'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Mercy]] is prohibited from returning to the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c20">''Underlord'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Akura Charity]] promised to add a gift to the [[Blackflame Empire]] in compensation.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c21">''Underlord'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Charity]] addresses the people of the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Charity]] did not select [[Lindon]] as one of the three competitors from the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c21" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] serves as the ancestral home of [[Orthos]]'s [[dragon]] lineage and shares a cultural origin with the people who named [[Ninecloud]] country. While major families seek to claim the empire, its [[fate]] remains tied to the [[Uncrowned King]] tournament where it competes as a vassal team of the [[Akura clan]]. During the competition in [[Night Wheel Valley]], the empire successfully doubled its number of Underlords, though it remains weaker than the [[Frozen Blade school]] and is dwarfed by [[Akura clan]] power. As a vassal team currently en route to the tournament, the empire includes representatives such as [[Yerin Arelius]] and contains descendants of [[House Arelius]], including [[Eithan]] Arelius. During the matches, a fighter from the empire is pitted against the final descendant of their original royal family.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">17</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is the empire from which [[Orthos]]'s [[dragon]] ancestors descend.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c1">''Uncrowned'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] competitors have already been chosen.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c2">''Uncrowned'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The major families want the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c4">''Uncrowned'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] doubled its number of Underlords during the competition in the [[Night Wheel Valley]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura clan]]'s power dwarfs the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c8">''Uncrowned'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[fate]] of the [[Blackflame Empire]] is tied to the [[Uncrowned King]] tournament.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a vassal team in the [[Uncrowned King]] tournament.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c9">''Uncrowned'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is closer to the [[dragons]] and weaker than the [[Frozen Blade school]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] team is en route.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a group that [[Lindon]] wants to keep out of [[dragon]] hands.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The culture that named the [[Ninecloud]] country is the same as the one that named the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c10">''Uncrowned'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a vassal team of the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Naru Saeya]] wants the name of the [[Blackflame Empire]] to spread.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15">''Uncrowned'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|A fighter from the [[Blackflame Empire]] is matched against the last descendant of their original royal family.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c16">''Uncrowned'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] contains descendants of [[House Arelius]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c17">''Uncrowned'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is the faction/location of [[Eithan]] Arelius.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c18">''Uncrowned'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Yerin Arelius]] is a representative of the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c19">''Uncrowned'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] is a significant territory that [[Eithan]] spent much of his [[life]] inhabiting, though he now seeks to prevent the empire from launching an invasion. The faction contains [[sacred artists]] who were transported to the [[Night Wheel Valley]] and employs workers from the [[Arelius family]]. During a period of training intended to prepare for an invasion, the empire was targeted by [[Redmoon Emissaries]] and faced the necessity of evacuation before the arrival of the [[Dreadgods]]. While a group is expected to emerge in the western portion of the empire, the territory has also been linked to the humiliation and death of [[Kiro]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">13</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a target of interest.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2">''Wintersteel'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Eithan]] spent much of his [[life]] in the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is an entity that [[Eithan]] seeks to prevent from invading.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c4">''Wintersteel'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a territory that would be destroyed by the [[Dreadgods]]' invasion.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c5">''Wintersteel'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a faction or empire mentioned in relation to [[Eithan]]'s clothing.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7">''Wintersteel'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] contains [[sacred artists]] who have been transported to the [[Night Wheel Valley]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a factor in [[Yerin]]'s training.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8">''Wintersteel'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is an entity that people are training to stop an invasion from.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9">''Wintersteel'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] needs to be evacuated before the danger of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c11">''Wintersteel'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] was targeted by [[Redmoon Emissaries]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] has [[Arelius family]] workers.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c15">''Wintersteel'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The group will emerge in the western [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c18">''Wintersteel'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blackflame Empire]] humiliated [[Kiro]] before killing him.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c20">''Wintersteel'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Located beyond the [[Desolate Wilds]], the [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] serves as a protective domain that encompasses the western region of [[Orthos]]. During its history, the empire has been the site where the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] was witnessed, yet it lacks an [[Archlord]] to rule over its territory. While the empire is considered too weak to attract the interest of the [[Wandering Titan]], it has faced internal instability as the [[Jai clan]] was pushed out of its proper lands.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">7</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Blackflame Empire]] provides protection to people.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2">''Bloodline'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a location containing the western region where [[Orthos]] is located.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c5">''Bloodline'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] was witnessed in the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9">''Bloodline'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|There is no [[Archlord]] in the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10">''Bloodline'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Blackflame Empire]] lies beyond the [[Desolate Wilds]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is considered too weak to interest the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Jai clan]] was being pushed out of the [[Blackflame Empire]] proper.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21">''Bloodline'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] is a massive territory protected by the [[Wastelanders]]. A fleet of cloudships once arrived from this vast domain, which maintains a complex hierarchy of subordinates. Within the empire, clerks and administrators manage recruitment efforts, though nearly every [[sacred artist]] faces a significant barrier when attempting to advance to the [[Underlord]] stage. While [[Malice]] has forces arrayed around its borders, the empire remains within striking range of the fighting between [[Monarchs]]. [[Jai Long]] has expressed a desire to avoid blending into this expansive location.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">9</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Wastelanders]] protect the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2">''Reaper'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A fleet of cloudships arrived from the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5">''Reaper'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is described as being very large.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Blackflame Empire]] has subordinates.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6">''Reaper'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is a location [[Jai Long]] wishes to avoid blending into.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Advancing to [[Underlord]] is a barrier for virtually every [[sacred artist]] in the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Malice]] has forces arrayed around the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10">''Reaper'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Blackflame Empire]] has clerks and administrators organizing recruitment.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c11">''Reaper'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is within [[Striker]] range of the [[Monarchs]]' fighting.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c20">''Reaper'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Located in [[Ashwind]], the [[Blackflame]] [[Empire]] was historically governed by black [[dragon]] emperors and later by the [[Blackflame family]]. The empire's northern coast is secured by a protective wall. During recent events, there was an expectation that a portal leading back to the empire would open.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is in [[Ashwind]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Blackflame Empire]] was once ruled by black [[dragon]] emperors.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c7">''Dreadgod'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Blackflame Empire]] has a wall protecting its northern coast.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c10">''Dreadgod'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Blackflame Empire]] was once ruled by the [[Blackflame family]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A portal back to the [[Blackflame Empire]] was expected to open.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24">''Dreadgod'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Waybound ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Refugees from the [[Blackflame Empire]] are mentioned.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The battle took place over the western [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c19">''Waybound'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The western [[Blackflame Empire]] is a possible starting location for [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c23">''Waybound'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is in a steady decline.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|In the [[Blackflame Empire]], [[Overlord]] scales were once considered the highest grade.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Blackflame Empire]] is an empire.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|“I am Jai Sen, of the Jai clan whose honor echoes in every corner of the Blackflame Empire.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c5" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|“In the name of the Blackflame Empire, and by the authority of the emperor, you are under arrest,” the old man said.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|“I will add a gift of my own to the Blackflame Empire in compensation,”<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c21" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|“People of the Blackflame Empire, congratulations.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c21" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|"I don’t want my name to spread, but the name of the Blackflame Empire. I need you to make that happen."<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle locations]]
[[Category:Nations and empires]]
